revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Burial Mounds''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient burial mounds located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Burial Mound|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This burial mound group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures qq1.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Burial Mounds with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The burial mounds were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Burial Mounds List == The following burial mounds are part of the Nakadoori Burial Mounds, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Burial Mound|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | qq1.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | qq.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** - Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** - Nakadoori Burial Mounds (Designated 1959).
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Items == * {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}} * {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} * {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}

{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Burial Mounds''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient burial mounds located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Burial Mound|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This burial mound group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures qq1.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Burial Mounds with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The burial mounds were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Burial Mounds List == The following burial mounds are part of the Nakadoori Burial Mounds, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Burial Mound|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | qq1.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | qq.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** - Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** - Nakadoori Burial Mounds (Designated 1959).

This burial mound group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures qq1.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Burial Mounds with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Burial Mounds (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The burial mounds were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Burial Mound|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}

{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures qq1.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=qq-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | qq1.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | qq.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** - Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** - Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).

{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** - Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** - Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Items == * {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}} * {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} * {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}

== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** - Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** - Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Items == * {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}} * {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} * {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}

{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** - Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** - Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).

{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** ** Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** ** Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).

== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** ** Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** ** Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Items == * {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}} * {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} * {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** ** Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** ** Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related Items == * {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏}} * {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社}} * {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社}}

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=draft}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.
== Excavation History == * **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} * **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties. * **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} * **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
== Kofuns List == The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} | Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=draft}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties | | Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - | | Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Important Cultural Property:** ** Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019). * **Historic Site:** ** Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).
== References == {{reflist}}

{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|ja|阿蘇市|12=draft}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=draft}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.
== Overview ==
This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|ja|阿蘇カルデラ|12=draft}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.
Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|ja|大野窟古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Hikawa Town|ja|氷川町|12=draft}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso Clan|ja|阿蘇氏|12=draft}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|ja|阿蘇神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=draft}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:中通古墳群 up to rev 99995880
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''中通古墳群'''（なかどおりこふんぐん）は、[[熊本県]][[阿蘇市]]一の宮町中通にある[[古墳群]]。熊本県指定史跡に指定され、[[長目塚古墳]]出土品は熊本県指定重要文化財に指定されている。
== 概要 == 熊本県北東部、阿蘇地方の[[阿蘇カルデラ]]北東部の東岳川流域に築造された古墳群である。[[1949年|1949]]-[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]24-25年）の東岳川改修の際に調査が実施されている{{Sfn|中通古墳群（平凡社）|1985}}{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。
現在は[[前方後円墳]]2基・[[円墳]]8基の計10基が認められるが、かつては14基以上から構成されたという{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。主墳の[[長目塚古墳]]は墳丘長111.5メートルを測る大型前方後円墳で、熊本県では[[大野窟古墳]]（[[八代郡]][[氷川町]]、123メートル）とともに最大級の規模になる。前述の調査の際には長目塚古墳で前方部の発掘調査が実施され、石室と人骨・副葬品が検出されている（詳細は「[[長目塚古墳]]」を参照）{{Sfn|中通古墳群（平凡社）|1985}}。営造時期は[[5世紀]]から[[6世紀]]頃と推定される{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}。古代には[[阿蘇君|阿蘇君（阿蘇氏）]]一族が阿蘇地方を治めたことが知られ、中通古墳群をその墓に比定する説がある{{Sfn|中通古墳群（平凡社）|1985}}{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}。
古墳域は[[1959年]]（昭和34年）に熊本県指定史跡に指定されたほか、長目塚古墳の出土品は[[2019年]]（[[平成]]31年）に熊本県指定重要文化財に指定されている<ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。なお周辺では、同様に阿蘇氏に関係する[[式内社]]として[[阿蘇神社]]や[[国造神社]]の鎮座も知られる。
== 遺跡歴 == * [[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）、中村徳五郎が九州日日新聞において調査報告{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1924年]]（[[大正]]13年）、中村徳五郎が『歴史地理』において調査報告{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1949年|1949]]-[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]24-25年）、東岳川の河川改修に際する事前調査。各古墳の測量調査および長目塚古墳前方部の発掘調査（乙益重隆ら。[[1962年]]（昭和37年）に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1959年]]（昭和34年）12月8日、熊本県指定史跡に指定<ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）、圃場整備工事に際して車塚A号墳付近で竪穴式石室の発見{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、測量調査（熊本大学。1994年（平成6年）に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[2007年]]（平成19年）3月28日、長目塚古墳出土品が阿蘇市指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[2010年|2010]]-[[2013年]]度（平成22-25年度）、長目塚古墳出土品の再整理・分析（熊本大学文学部。[[2014年]]（平成26年）に報告書刊行）。 * [[2019年]]（平成31年）3月26日、長目塚古墳出土品が熊本県指定重要文化財に指定<ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。
== 一覧 == 現在認められる古墳は次の通り（2014年（平成26年）の『長目塚古墳の研究』 p. 14掲載の分布図に基づく。規模の値は1949年（昭和24年）の報告値{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}）。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+中通古墳群の一覧{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}} !古墳名 !座標 !形状 !墳丘長 !高さ !備考 |- |鞍掛塚A号墳||{{Coord|32|58|10.51|N|131|6|12.62|E|region:JP-43_type:landmark|display=inline|name=鞍掛塚A号墳}}||円墳||33.0m||4.4m|| |- |乳母塚古墳||{{Coord|32|57|56.07|N|131|6|24.44|E|region:JP-43_type:landmark|display=inline|name=乳母塚古墳（消滅）}}|| || || ||消滅古墳 |- |車塚古墳横の石室||{{Coord|32|57|55.89|N|131|6|31.70|E|region:JP-43_type:landmark|display=inline|name=車塚古墳横の石室（消滅）}}|| || || ||消滅古墳 |- == 文化財 == === 熊本県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** 長目塚出土品 9種451点（附 残欠一括）（考古資料） - 2019年（平成31年）3月26日指定<ref name="指定文化財一覧">[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural_property/ 指定文化財一覧]（阿蘇市ホームページ）。</ref>。 * 史跡 ** 中通古墳群 - 1959年（昭和34年）12月8日指定<ref name="熊本県指定史跡">[https://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/hpkiji/pub/List.aspx?c_id=3&class_set_id=1&class_id=1768 指定文化財]（熊本県ホームページ）上のファイル（2015年10月29日更新版）参照。</ref><ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[中通古墳群]] to [[Nakadori Kofun Group]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''中通古墳群'''（なかどおりこふんぐん）は、[[熊本県]][[阿蘇市]]一の宮町中通にある[[古墳群]]。熊本県指定史跡に指定され、[[長目塚古墳]]出土品は熊本県指定重要文化財に指定されている。
== 概要 == 熊本県北東部、阿蘇地方の[[阿蘇カルデラ]]北東部の東岳川流域に築造された古墳群である。[[1949年|1949]]-[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]24-25年）の東岳川改修の際に調査が実施されている{{Sfn|中通古墳群（平凡社）|1985}}{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。
現在は[[前方後円墳]]2基・[[円墳]]8基の計10基が認められるが、かつては14基以上から構成されたという{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。主墳の[[長目塚古墳]]は墳丘長111.5メートルを測る大型前方後円墳で、熊本県では[[大野窟古墳]]（[[八代郡]][[氷川町]]、123メートル）とともに最大級の規模になる。前述の調査の際には長目塚古墳で前方部の発掘調査が実施され、石室と人骨・副葬品が検出されている（詳細は「[[長目塚古墳]]」を参照）{{Sfn|中通古墳群（平凡社）|1985}}。営造時期は[[5世紀]]から[[6世紀]]頃と推定される{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}。古代には[[阿蘇君|阿蘇君（阿蘇氏）]]一族が阿蘇地方を治めたことが知られ、中通古墳群をその墓に比定する説がある{{Sfn|中通古墳群（平凡社）|1985}}{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}。
古墳域は[[1959年]]（昭和34年）に熊本県指定史跡に指定されたほか、長目塚古墳の出土品は[[2019年]]（[[平成]]31年）に熊本県指定重要文化財に指定されている<ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。なお周辺では、同様に阿蘇氏に関係する[[式内社]]として[[阿蘇神社]]や[[国造神社]]の鎮座も知られる。
== 遺跡歴 == * [[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）、中村徳五郎が九州日日新聞において調査報告{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1924年]]（[[大正]]13年）、中村徳五郎が『歴史地理』において調査報告{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1949年|1949]]-[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]24-25年）、東岳川の河川改修に際する事前調査。各古墳の測量調査および長目塚古墳前方部の発掘調査（乙益重隆ら。[[1962年]]（昭和37年）に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1959年]]（昭和34年）12月8日、熊本県指定史跡に指定<ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）、圃場整備工事に際して車塚A号墳付近で竪穴式石室の発見{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、測量調査（熊本大学。1994年（平成6年）に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}}。 * [[2007年]]（平成19年）3月28日、長目塚古墳出土品が阿蘇市指定有形文化財に指定。 * [[2010年|2010]]-[[2013年]]度（平成22-25年度）、長目塚古墳出土品の再整理・分析（熊本大学文学部。[[2014年]]（平成26年）に報告書刊行）。 * [[2019年]]（平成31年）3月26日、長目塚古墳出土品が熊本県指定重要文化財に指定<ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。
== 一覧 == 現在認められる古墳は次の通り（2014年（平成26年）の『長目塚古墳の研究』 p. 14掲載の分布図に基づく。規模の値は1949年（昭和24年）の報告値{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}）。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+中通古墳群の一覧{{Sfn|広報あそ 2007年12月号|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|長目塚古墳の研究|2014|pp=11-22}} !古墳名 !座標 !形状 !墳丘長 !高さ !備考 |- == 文化財 == === 熊本県指定文化財 === * 重要文化財（有形文化財） ** 長目塚出土品 9種451点（附 残欠一括）（考古資料） - 2019年（平成31年）3月26日指定<ref name="指定文化財一覧">[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural_property/ 指定文化財一覧]（阿蘇市ホームページ）。</ref>。 * 史跡 ** 中通古墳群 - 1959年（昭和34年）12月8日指定<ref name="熊本県指定史跡">[https://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/hpkiji/pub/List.aspx?c_id=3&class_set_id=1&class_id=1768 指定文化財]（熊本県ホームページ）上のファイル（2015年10月29日更新版）参照。</ref><ref name="指定文化財一覧"/>。

== 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1962|chapter=|title=阿蘇長目塚 付 小嵐山古墳（熊本県文化財調査報告 第3集）|publisher=熊本県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=中通古墳群|title=熊本大学文学部考古学研究室研究報告 第1集|publisher=熊本大学文学部考古学研究室|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2021|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115207 中通古墳群を考える -長目塚古墳の温故知新-（長目塚古墳発掘70周年・熊本県史跡指定60周年・出土品熊本県重要文化財指定記念シンポジウム記録集）]|publisher=阿蘇市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。
== 関連項目 == * [[阿蘇国造]] * [[阿蘇氏]] * [[阿蘇神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/tourism/spot/historic/remains/ 古墳・遺跡 > 中通古墳群] - 阿蘇市ホームページ

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:中通古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|32.96735278|N|131.10660833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28688675]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}}
{{GeoGroup}}
'''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in [[:en:Aso, Kumamoto|Aso, Kumamoto]], Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|qq=|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.

== Overview ==

This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the [[:en:Aso Caldera|Aso Caldera]]. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]] in [[:en:Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa Town]], which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the [[:en:Aso clan|Aso Clan]], who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the [[:en:Aso Shrine|Aso Shrine]] and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|qq=|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

== Excavation History ==
* **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
* **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties.
* **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}

== Kofuns List ==
The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
| Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|qq=|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties |
| Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - |
| Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties ===
* **Important Cultural Property:**
** Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019).
* **Historic Site:**
** Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).

== References ==
{{reflist}}

== Related Items ==
* [[:en:Aso clan|Aso Clan]]
* [[:en:Aso Shrine|Aso Shrine]]
* {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|qq=|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}

{{translated page|ja|中通古墳群|version=99995880|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Kofun clusters]]
}}

'''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in [[:en:Aso, Kumamoto|Aso, Kumamoto]], Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.

== Overview ==

This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the [[:en:Aso Caldera|Aso Caldera]]. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]] in [[:en:Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa Town]], which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the [[:en:Aso clan|Aso Clan]], who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the [[:en:Aso Shrine|Aso Shrine]] and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

== Excavation History ==
* **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
* **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties.
* **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}

Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]] in [[:en:Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa Town]], which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the [[:en:Aso clan|Aso Clan]], who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the [[:en:Aso Shrine|Aso Shrine]] and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

== Excavation History ==
* **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
* **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties.
* **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}

{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}}
{{GeoGroup}}
'''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in [[:en:Aso, Kumamoto|Aso, Kumamoto]], Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.

== Overview ==

This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the [[:en:Aso Caldera|Aso Caldera]]. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]] in [[:en:Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa Town]], which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the [[:en:Aso clan|Aso Clan]], who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the [[:en:Aso Shrine|Aso Shrine]] and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

== Excavation History ==
* **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
* **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties.
* **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}

== Kofuns List ==
The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
| Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties |
| Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - |
| Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties ===
* **Important Cultural Property:**
** Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019).
* **Historic Site:**
** Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).

The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the [[:en:Aso clan|Aso Clan]], who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the [[:en:Aso Shrine|Aso Shrine]] and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

== Excavation History ==
* **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
* **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties.
* **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}

Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.

== Overview ==

This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the [[:en:Aso Caldera|Aso Caldera]]. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the [[:en:Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun]] in [[:en:Hikawa, Kumamoto|Hikawa Town]], which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the [[:en:Aso clan|Aso Clan]], who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.
