{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|32.96735278|N|131.10660833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28688675]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|lat_deg=32|lat_min=58|lat_sec=2.47|lon_deg=131|lon_min=6|lon_sec=23.79|label=中通古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|中通古墳群の位置}}}}
{{GeoGroup}}
'''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|en|Aso, Kumamoto|WD=Q733681|lt=Aso, Kumamoto}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.

== Overview ==

This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|en|Aso Caldera|WD=Q11657618|lt=Aso Caldera}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|en|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|WD=Q24876586|lt=Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun}} in {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa Town}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso clan|en|Aso clan|WD=Q11657653|lt=Aso Clan}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|en|Aso Shrine|WD=Q715639|lt=Aso Shrine}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

== Excavation History ==
* **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
* **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties.
* **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}

== Kofuns List ==
The following Kofuns are part of the Nakadoori Kofuns, based on the 2014 report on Nagamezuka.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
| Mound Name | Coordinates | Shape | Length | Height | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} | {{Coord|32|58|2.47|N|131|6|23.79|E}} | Keyhole | 111.5m | 9.9m | Artifacts designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties |
| Shōbuzuka | {{Coord|32|58|3.04|N|131|6|32.42|E}} | Round | 58.7m | 11.4m | - |
| Kurazuka A | {{Coord|32|57|52.51|N|131|6|31.16|E}} | Round | 47.0m | 8.0m | - |

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Kumamoto Prefectural Cultural Properties ===
* **Important Cultural Property:**
** Artifacts from Nagamezuka (451 items, including remnants) (Designated 2019).
* **Historic Site:**
** Nakadoori Kofuns (Designated 1959).

'''Nakadoori Kofuns''' (中通古墳群) are a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Aso, Kumamoto|en|Aso, Kumamoto|WD=Q733681|lt=Aso, Kumamoto|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}, Japan. Designated as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site, artifacts from the {{ill|Nagamezuka Kofun|ja|長目塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamezuka Kofun|1=Nagamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688678}} are also designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.

== Overview ==

This Kofun group is located in the northeastern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, in the Aso region, near the Higashidake River in the northeastern section of the {{ill|Aso Caldera|en|Aso Caldera|WD=Q11657618|lt=Aso Caldera|1=Aso Caldera}}. Investigations were conducted during river improvement works on the Higashidake River between 1949 and 1950 (Showa 24–25).{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}.

Currently, ten mounds are identified, including two keyhole-shaped mounds and eight round mounds. It is believed that the site originally consisted of over 14 mounds.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}} The main mound, Nagamezuka, measures 111.5 meters in length, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped mounds in Kumamoto Prefecture, along with the {{ill|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|en|Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|WD=Q24876586|lt=Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun|1=Ōno-no-Iwa Kofun}} in {{ill|Hikawa, Kumamoto|en|Hikawa, Kumamoto|WD=Q1184800|lt=Hikawa Town|1=Hikawa, Kumamoto}}, which measures 123 meters. Excavations at Nagamezuka revealed a stone chamber, human bones, and burial artifacts. The site is estimated to date back to the 5th to 6th centuries.{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}} Some theories associate the Nakadoori Kofuns with the tombs of the {{ill|Aso clan|en|Aso clan|WD=Q11657653|lt=Aso Clan|1=Aso clan}}, who historically ruled the Aso region.{{Sfn|Nakadoori Kofuns (Heibonsha)|1985}}{{Sfn|Aso City Bulletin December 2007|2007|p=23}}.

The Kofuns were designated a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 (Showa 34), and the artifacts from Nagamezuka were designated as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties in 2019 (Heisei 31).{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}} Nearby, the {{ill|Aso Shrine|en|Aso Shrine|WD=Q715639|lt=Aso Shrine|1=Aso Shrine}} and {{ill|Kunizo Shrine|ja|国造神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunizo Shrine|1=Kokuzō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421390}}, related to the Aso Clan, are also notable sacred sites.

== Excavation History ==
* **1903 (Meiji 36):** Research by Nakamura Tokugorō reported in the Kyūshū Nichinichi Shimbun.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1924 (Taishō 13):** Further research by Nakamura Tokugorō published in ''Rekishi Chiri''.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1949–1950 (Showa 24–25):** Preliminary surveys and excavations during the Higashidake River improvement project.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1959 (Showa 34):** Designation as a Kumamoto Prefectural Historic Site.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
* **1973 (Showa 48):** Discovery of a pit-style stone chamber near Kurazuka A Mound during land improvement works.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **1989 (Heisei 1):** Survey by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2007 (Heisei 19):** Nagamezuka artifacts designated as Aso City Tangible Cultural Properties.
* **2010–2013 (Heisei 22–25):** Reanalysis and reorganization of Nagamezuka artifacts by Kumamoto University.{{Sfn|Research on Nagamezuka Kofun|2014|pp=11-22}}
* **2019 (Heisei 31):** Designation of Nagamezuka artifacts as Kumamoto Prefectural Important Cultural Properties.{{Sfn|Cultural Properties List}}
